changes.citation: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:7 — change_type: DOCS. Valid values per SOP v1.3.1: CONTENT | BEHAVIOUR | MIXED. DOCS is not a valid value; kernel preflight will reject. All expected outputs are documentation under /docs/; correct value is CONTENT.
changes.resolution: GPT must correct change_type to CONTENT and reissue the prompt. Alternatively GPT may elect the Docs-Only Bypass (SOP §11) — all expected changes are under /docs/ so bypass is eligible; if bypass is used, no front matter or bus lifecycle is required.

changes.note: Docs-only sprint. No Intelligence Core components, no governed code, no SSOT, no pipeline files in scope. HIGH is an overstatement — STANDARD is appropriate. Conservative and not a kernel blocker, but triggers unnecessarily heavy governance (Claude audit + GPT review + dual approval). GPT should confirm intent.

fields_confirmed: CONFIRMED: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:4 — risk_level: HIGH (valid value, conservative — see WARN above)

changes.duplicate_authority_sources: NONE detected. LAYER_ARCHITECTURE_AUTHORITY_INDEX r2 supersedes the original by naming convention; no conflict.

findings.claim: Strategic Vision v1.5 §2.3 defines Layer A/B/C model
findings.citation: docs/strategy/HealthIQ_AI_Strategic-Vision-and-12-Month-Sprint-Plan_v1.5_FINAL_ADOPTED.md lines 91-145 — Layer A (canonicalisation/governed inputs), Layer B (deterministic analytical engine including signal activation, phenotype mapping, WHY, clinician reporting), Layer C (narrative translation and presentation only). Exactly as described in prompt.

findings.claim: ADR-005 uses A/B/C/D stage labels that conflict with product Layer vocabulary
findings.citation: architecture/ADR-005-disease-specific-signal-evaluation-v2.md lines 79-105 — Stage 3 is explicitly labelled 'Layer C: Signal Evaluation Engine'. In product terms, Signal Evaluation is Layer B work. Stage 4 is 'Layer D: Insight and Narrative Construction'. These are local pipeline stage labels, not product-level Layer vocabulary. Drift is real and reconciliation sprint is justified.

findings.claim: NarrativePayloadV1 is the governed Layer B → Layer C handoff object
findings.citation: backend/core/contracts/narrative_payload_v1.py line 20: NARRATIVE_PAYLOAD_SCHEMA_VERSION = 'v1.1'. Docstring lines 2-6 confirms WP2/LAYER-B-1 formal handoff object. Governed DTO contract exists in repo.

findings.claim: ADR-002 defines three-layer architecture with Layer C = Narrative Translation
findings.citation: architecture/ADR-002-deterministic-analysis-engine.md lines 92-108 — Layer C defined as 'Narrative Translation'. Line 107-108: 'Any computation performed in Layer C that belongs in Layer B is an architectural defect and must be treated as a blocking governance violation.' Aligns with Strategic Vision v1.5 §2.3.

changes.note: change_type should be CONTENT (docs-only). No Intelligence Core components, no analytical code, no output generation changes in scope. No behavioural hardening required.

Prompt contains six explicit Do Not clauses: no runtime code, no tests, no frontend behaviour, no backend compilers, no Gemini implementation, no Layer B/C runtime logic. STOP conditions are adequate for the documentation scope.

notes: New ADR output path is docs/architecture/ADR-LAYER-BOUNDARY-RECONCILIATION-1.md. Existing canonical ADRs live in architecture/ (not docs/architecture/). Cursor must follow prompt instruction to explain any path deviation in the audit summary.
AUTHORITY_MAP.md update is optional per prompt. Architecture section lists ADRs — minimal entry addition is appropriate if Cursor elects to update it.
Docs-Only Bypass (SOP §11) is available: all expected outputs are under /docs/. If GPT elects bypass, no bus lifecycle is required.

overall_verdict: NOT_HARDENED — one BLOCKER: change_type: DOCS is not a valid SOP v1.3.1 value. GPT must correct to CONTENT (or elect Docs-Only Bypass) and reissue the prompt before Cursor may proceed.
